raw_helios.reference_name: Bespalov et al., PRL supplementary Fig. S5 ambient Al cross-check

notes: Points were transcribed from the supplied PDF/screenshot set and cleaned into monotonic k-ordered series.
This is cleaner and better documented than the earlier in-code scaffold, but still not publication-grade digitization.

warnings: k*lambda_D >= 1, so the selected state is in a non-collective scattering regime.

raw_helios.reference_name: Bespalov et al., driven Al dispersion comparison (experiment/TDDFT/LFC/RPA)

reference_provenance.figure: Main-text / supplement driven Al dispersion comparison
notes: The benchmark state targets the approximately 6.3 ns probe-time compressed Al slab with density 3.75-4.5 g/cm^3 and Te about 0.6 eV.
Points remain manually digitized, but the provenance, geometry, and state assumptions are now explicit and script-consumable.

article_al_benchmark.reference_name: Bespalov et al., driven Al dispersion comparison (experiment/TDDFT/LFC/RPA)

reference_provenance.figure: Main-text / supplement driven Al dispersion comparison
notes: The benchmark state targets the approximately 6.3 ns probe-time compressed Al slab with density 3.75-4.5 g/cm^3 and Te about 0.6 eV.
Points remain manually digitized, but the provenance, geometry, and state assumptions are now explicit and script-consumable.

benchmark_valence_aware.reference_name: Bespalov et al., driven Al dispersion comparison (experiment/TDDFT/LFC/RPA)

reference_provenance.figure: Main-text / supplement driven Al dispersion comparison
notes: The benchmark state targets the approximately 6.3 ns probe-time compressed Al slab with density 3.75-4.5 g/cm^3 and Te about 0.6 eV.
Points remain manually digitized, but the provenance, geometry, and state assumptions are now explicit and script-consumable.

notes: The benchmark state targets the approximately 6.3 ns probe-time compressed Al slab with density 3.75-4.5 g/cm^3 and Te about 0.6 eV.
Points remain manually digitized, but the provenance, geometry, and state assumptions are now explicit and script-consumable.
